# Test Quality Review — `lib/classes/` + `test/classes/`

**Scope:** All non-generated source files under `lib/classes/` and their corresponding test files
under `test/classes/`.

**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

---

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/day_plan_test.dart` is 735 lines — the largest test file in scope. The Glados generator boilerplate (`_GeneratedDayDate`, `_GeneratedDayPlanBlocks`, extension `_AnyDayPlan`) occupies lines 599–735 (136 lines). Consider extracting the generators into a private section clearly delineated from the test groups; the file itself does not need splitting but a structural separator improves navigation.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/project_data_test.dart` (505 L) and `test/classes/project_entity_test.dart` (477 L) both test classes defined in `lib/classes/project_data.dart`. The "one test file per source file" rule (AGENTS.md) is violated. The `_GeneratedProjectStatusKind` enum, `_projectStatus` factory, `_projectDate`/`_projectEntityDate` helpers, `_optionalProjectText`, and `_vectorClock` are duplicated near-verbatim (`project_entity_test.dart:441`–`477` vs `project_data_test.dart:488`–`505`). Merge into a single `project_data_test.dart`.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/entry_link_collapsed_test.dart` should be renamed `entry_link_test.dart` to follow the one-test-file naming convention. The current name is artifact-scoped (the field that prompted the test), not source-file-scoped.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/rating_data_test.dart` is 647 lines. Lines 556–648 are generator boilerplate. No split needed, but the file length is driven largely by redundant static cases (see Test Quality section).

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/classes/task_test.dart` — **`TaskData` and `TaskStatus` have no JSON round-trip tests.** The file has 14 test cases, all exercising `copyWith` and language-code preservation. `TaskData.fromJson`/`toJson`, `TaskStatus.fromJson`/`toJson`, the full `statusHistory` list serialization, and the `@Default` / `@JsonKey(unknownEnumValue)` for `languageSource` and `priority` are completely untested. This is a significant gap given how central `TaskData` is to the domain.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/classes/journal_entities_test.dart` — Only 4 of 16 `JournalEntity.affectedIds` switch branches are tested (`checklistItem`, `dayPlan`, `checklist`, `journalEntry`). The remaining 12 branches (`journalImage`, `journalAudio`, `task`, `event`, `quantitative`, `measurement`, `aiResponse`, `workout`, `habitCompletion`, `survey`, `rating`, `project`) are not exercised, leaving dead branches that could regress silently.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/classes/entity_definitions_test.dart` — The `EntityDefinition` sealed union (5 variants) and `DashboardItem` sealed union (5 variants) have **no tests at all** in this file. Only `ChecklistCorrectionExample` and `AutoCompleteRule` are tested. `MeasurableDataType`, `CategoryDefinition`, `LabelDefinition`, `HabitDefinition`, `DashboardDefinition`, and all five `DashboardItem` variants are missing JSON round-trip and equality tests.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/task_test.dart` lines 22–141 — six `test(...)` cases for `copyWith` with `languageCode` (lines 50–64, 87–103, 105–121, 123–141) are highly repetitive. Each constructs a `TaskData`, calls `copyWith`, and asserts a single field. These can be collapsed into one or two parameterized helpers. The pattern `copyWith supports language code` / `copyWith preserves language code` / `copyWith can update language code` / `copyWith can clear language code` tests the same contract with minor variation.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/rating_data_test.dart` — The static tests `serializes to and from JSON` (line 330), `JSON round-trip preserves all fields` (line 347), `serializes without optional note` (line 372), and `JSON round-trip preserves catalogId` (line 421) overlap substantially with what the Glados property at line 437 already covers. Once the Glados test exists, these static cases add marginal value and inflate the file to 647 lines.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/project_data_test.dart` + `test/classes/project_entity_test.dart` — `_GeneratedProjectStatusKind` enum is declared identically in both files (`project_data_test.dart:271`, `project_entity_test.dart:214`). The `_projectStatus` / `_projectEntityDate` / `_optionalProjectText` / `_vectorClock` helpers are also duplicated. DRY violation; should live in one file after the merge described in File Size.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/checklist_item_data_test.dart:126` — The assertion `expect(json['checkedAt'], data.checkedAt?.toIso8601String())` checks the pre-encode value against the original, but not the round-tripped `restored` object. The test at line 119 (`expect(restored, equals(data))`) covers overall equality, but the individual field assertion on the decoded object is missing: should be `expect(restored.checkedAt, data.checkedAt)`.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/day_plan_test.dart` line 424 — `status helper methods work correctly` tests `isAgreed`, `isDraft`, `needsReview` but not `isCommitted` (the `DayPlanStatusCommitted` variant added in the impl at `day_plan.dart:89`). The `isCommitted` getter is only implied by Glados indirectly.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/classes/task.dart` — **No Glados round-trip for `TaskData` or `TaskStatus`**. `TaskData` has complex fields: `statusHistory: List<TaskStatus>`, `aiSuppressedLabelIds: Set<String>`, `@Default` and `@JsonKey(unknownEnumValue)` on `languageSource`, `@Default` on `priority`, and a `Duration?` estimate. This is prime Glados territory: generate random `TaskData` trees, round-trip through JSON, assert `decoded == original`. `TaskStatus` (7 union variants, each with `Geolocation?`) equally warrants a generative round-trip test.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/classes/journal_entities.dart` — `Metadata` (the metadata struct shared by every `JournalEntity` variant) has no Glados round-trip test anywhere in `test/classes/`. With optional `labelIds`, `VectorClock?`, `EntryFlag?`, `categoryId`, and both nullable `DateTime` fields, it has rich input space. A generative test in `journal_entities_test.dart` would cover the most-used serialization path in the entire codebase.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/classes/entity_definitions.dart` — `EntityDefinition` (5 union variants including `DashboardDefinition` with a `List<DashboardItem>`) and `DashboardItem` (5 union variants) are untested entirely. Both are composite, recursive-enough structures where property-based round-trip tests would add real value.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/health.dart` — `QuantitativeData` sealed union (`cumulativeQuantityData`, `discreteQuantityData`) has no test coverage. A two-variant Glados round-trip would be straightforward and catch any `platformType`/`sourceName` optional-field regressions.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/geolocation.dart` — `Geolocation` is embedded in `ImageData`, `AudioData`, `Metadata`, `TaskStatus`, and `ProjectStatus`. Its own JSON serialization (11 fields, 7 of which are optional `double?`) is never tested directly. A Glados round-trip would be small (combine4 of date + lat/lon + geohash slots).

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/event_status.dart` — `EventStatusX.label` (8 branches) and `EventStatusX.color` (8 branches) have no generative coverage. A `Glados(any.choose(EventStatus.values))` round-trip for `label` and a light static test for `color` would give full coverage cheaply.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/checklist_data.dart` — `ChecklistData.fromJson`/`toJson` with variable-length `linkedChecklistItems` and `linkedTasks` lists is a natural Glados target, but has **no test file at all**.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/classes/entry_text.dart` — `EntryText` with optional `markdown`/`quill` fields has no test. A short Glados round-trip would be simple (combine3 slots for optional strings).

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/classes/event_data.dart` — `EventData.fromJson`/`toJson` with `EventStatus` enum and `double stars` has no test. A 3-variant static test plus a `Glados(any.choose(EventStatus.values))` would give full coverage.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/classes/journal_entities.dart` — `JournalEntityExtension.affectedIds` switch at lines 232–287 has 16 cases; only 4 are tested. The following branches are unexercised: `JournalImage` (→ `imageNotification`), `JournalAudio` (→ `audioNotification`), `Task` (→ `taskNotification`), `JournalEvent` (→ `eventNotification`), `QuantitativeEntry` (→ `data.dataType`), `SurveyEntry` (→ `surveyNotification`), `HabitCompletionEntry` (→ `habitId` + `habitCompletionNotification`), `WorkoutEntry` (→ `workoutNotification` + `workout.data.workoutType`), `MeasurementEntry` (→ `measurement.data.dataTypeId`), `AiResponseEntry` (→ `aiResponseNotification`), `RatingEntry` (→ `ratingNotification`), and `ProjectEntry` (→ `projectNotification`).

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/classes/entity_definitions.dart` — `EntityDefinition` sealed union's 5 variants have zero test coverage. Particularly `DashboardDefinition` (complex, with `days: @Default(30)` and nested `List<DashboardItem>`) is a regression risk.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `lib/classes/task.dart:taskStatusFromString` — The function calls `DateTime.now()` internally to construct timestamps. The tests assert `runtimeType` and `toDbString` (correct) but never assert that the produced status has valid fields (non-null `id`, non-zero `utcOffset`) or round-trips through JSON. Specifically, the `TaskStatus.blocked` and `TaskStatus.onHold` default reason text (`'needs a reason'`) is tested, but the JSON round-trip for these status variants — including the `reason` field — is never validated.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/day_plan.dart` — `DayPlanStatusCommitted` (the fourth status variant, lines 89–95): no named static test for its `isCommitted` property on `DayPlanData`. The only coverage is implicit via Glados, which does not generate this variant (the Glados test uses `const DayPlanStatus.draft()` as a fixed status).

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/notification_entity.dart` — `NotificationMeta` itself has no independent round-trip test. It is only tested embedded inside `NotificationEntity`. A standalone test for `NotificationMeta.fromJson` would confirm the `scheduledFor`, `vectorClock` (non-optional here), and all optional `DateTime?` fields serialize correctly.

- [ ] **[MED]** `lib/classes/supported_language.dart` — `localizedName(BuildContext)` is untested. While this requires a widget test, there is no widget test at all for this class. Worth noting as a gap even if addressed elsewhere.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/classes/entry_link.dart` — `EntryLink.rating` variant has only Glados coverage; no dedicated static test. The file is currently named `entry_link_collapsed_test.dart`, which implies only the `collapsed` field was tested — the full variant matrix (`basic`, `rating`, `project`) is only reached via Glados.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `lib/classes/rating_question.dart` — `copyWith` with `options: null` (clearing options) is not tested. The current copyWith test (line 119) updates `question` but never clears `options`.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/rating_data_test.dart` lines 211–222 and 437–451 — two independent Glados tests (`generatedRatingDimension` numRuns=160, `generatedRatingData` numRuns=160) = **320 runs** per CI invocation. The `generatedRatingData` test already generates nested `_GeneratedRatingDimension` lists, making it a strict superset of the dimension test. Reducing `generatedRatingDimension` to 80 runs (it is covered by the data-level test) saves ~80 runs with no coverage loss.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/rating_question_test.dart` lines 29–41 and 146–163 — `generatedRatingQuestionOption` (numRuns=140) is fully subsumed by `generatedRatingQuestion` (numRuns=160) because question generation embeds option generation. Reducing option numRuns to 60 saves ~80 CI runs.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/classes/entity_definitions_test.dart` lines 57–72 and 105–118 — two Glados tests totaling 140+160=300 runs. The `generatedAutoCompleteRule` test (160 runs) generates the compound `and`/`or`/`multiple` variants that embed two leaf rules each, so the `generatedChecklistCorrectionExample` test (140 runs) is not accelerated by the other — both are needed. However, `numRuns: 160` for autocomplete rules is high given their low-dimensional input space (8 int slots). Reducing to 100 saves 60 runs per CI invocation.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/day_plan_test.dart` lines 482–555 and 557–573 — two Glados tests with numRuns=140 and numRuns=120 = 260 runs. Both use identical `_GeneratedDayPlanBlocks` generator. The first test (invariants) and the second (round-trip) are distinct properties so both are needed, but the round-trip test at 120 could be reduced to 80 runs (it simply needs to hit all 9 block-count slots, which the generator covers quickly). Saves 40 runs.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/supported_language_test.dart` lines 81–91 — `generatedInvalidLanguageCode` (numRuns=160) tests a `choose` from 7 enum values × 7 kind values = 49 combinations; 160 runs is 3× the exhaustive space. Reducing to 50 saves 110 runs with identical coverage.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/classes/task_priority_test.dart` line 22 — `generatedTaskPriorityInput` (numRuns=160) samples from `4 priorities × 6 kinds × 3 case styles × 5 whitespace = 360` combinations; 160 runs covers most but not all. This is fine at 160. The second Glados test (`round-trips generated priorities through short labels`, numRuns=80) samples from 4 values — 80 runs is large; 20 would suffice for 4-element choose. Saves 60 runs.

**Total recoverable runs: approximately 390–490 runs** (from 3270 current total), with no meaningful coverage loss.

---

## Summary

- **Report path:** `test/classes/TEST_REVIEW.md`
- **Oversized impl files:** 0 — all non-generated impl files are under 330 lines; none exceed the 1000-line threshold. `entity_definitions.dart` at 327 lines is the largest and is appropriately sized.
- **Oversized test files:** 2 (`day_plan_test.dart` at 735 L, `rating_data_test.dart` at 647 L). Both are inflated by Glados boilerplate + redundant static cases, not by structural necessity.
- **Weak tests:** 4 files have meaningful quality issues — `task_test.dart` (no JSON round-trips for `TaskData`/`TaskStatus` at all), `journal_entities_test.dart` (12 of 16 `affectedIds` branches uncovered), `entity_definitions_test.dart` (`EntityDefinition` + `DashboardItem` unions entirely untested), and `project_data_test.dart`+`project_entity_test.dart` (two files for one source, with copy-pasted generator helpers).
- **Glados candidates (genuine gaps):** 7 — `TaskData`/`TaskStatus` (highest priority; central domain entity with zero JSON tests), `Metadata`, `EntityDefinition`+`DashboardItem`, `QuantitativeData`, `Geolocation`, `ChecklistData`, `EntryText`.
- **Speed wins:** 6 opportunities totaling ~390–490 recoverable runs from the current 3270 total (~12–15% reduction).
- **Biggest single opportunity:** Add JSON round-trip Glados tests for `TaskData` and `TaskStatus` in `task_test.dart`. These are the most-used domain entities in the app, they contain complex nested structures (`List<TaskStatus>`, `Set<String>`, two `@Default`+`@JsonKey` fields), and yet not a single `fromJson`/`toJson` call exists in their test file.
